channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Detroit Pistons
channel.description: Detroit Pistons trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: Duren: “I’ve just gotta be better, man. I have no …
item.description: ‪ ‪Omari Sankofa II‬: Duren: “I’ve just gotta be better, man. I have no excuses.”

item.title: JB Bickerstaff points out Duncan Robinson was being …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB Bickerstaff points out Duncan Robinson was being held coming off screens. “We’ll go back, we’ll watch it, we’ll send the clips in to the league, we’ll see what it is. But we have to play better.”

item.title: Game 4 vs. the Cavs was the fifth time Jalen Duren's …
item.description: ESPN Insights: Game 4 vs. the Cavs was the fifth time Jalen Duren's been held to single-digit points in the playoffs. He was held under 10 points in just six of his 70 games in the regular season.

item.title: Donovan Mitchell demolishes Pistons in the second half to even series
item.description: Danny Cunningham: #Cavs beat the Pistons 112-103 in a game that wasn't as close as the final score. Donovan Mitchell had one of his best games as a member of the Cavs, finishing with 43 points -- 39 of them in the 2nd half. Harden had 24 and hit five 3s. Series is tied at 2-2.

item.title: Game 4 vs. Detroit marks Donovan Mitchell's fourth …
item.description: ESPN Insight: Game 4 vs. Detroit marks Donovan Mitchell's fourth 40-point playoff game with the Cavaliers, passing Kyrie Irving for second-most in franchise history . Only LeBron James (24) has more.

item.title: JB Bickerstaff on what’s enabled Tobias Harris to …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB Bickerstaff on what’s enabled Tobias Harris to score 20+ in eight straight games: “His want to help this team and it’s as simple as that. He sees where a need is and he has the ability to fill that need.”

item.title: Caris LeVert is available, Kevin Huerter remains OUT
item.description: Jacob Richman: Caris LeVert is available, Kevin Huerter remains OUT.

item.title: The Cavaliers have listed Thomas Bryant (low back …
item.description: The Cavaliers have listed Thomas Bryant (low back spasms) as questionable for Monday's Game 4 against Detroit.

item.title: NBA Last Two Minute Report from Game 3 shows 0 errors. …
item.description: Chris Fedor: NBA Last Two Minute Report from Game 3 shows 0 errors. Tobias Harris’ late contest on James Harden’s 3-point dagger was clean, not considered a foul. “Harris jumps to contest Harden's shot, but would otherwise avoid making contact if not for Harden extending forward.”

item.title: Caris LeVert listed as questionable for Game 4 vs. Cavs
item.description: The Pistons have downgraded Caris LeVert (right heel contusion) to questionable for Monday’s Game 4 against Cleveland. Kevin Huerter (left adductor strain) is also questionable.

item.title: Duren: “I’ve just gotta be better, man. I have no …
item.description: ‪ ‪Omari Sankofa II‬: Duren: “I’ve just gotta be better, man. I have no excuses.”

item.title: JB Bickerstaff points out Duncan Robinson was being …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB Bickerstaff points out Duncan Robinson was being held coming off screens. “We’ll go back, we’ll watch it, we’ll send the clips in to the league, we’ll see what it is. But we have to play better.”

item.title: Game 4 vs. the Cavs was the fifth time Jalen Duren's …
item.description: ESPN Insights: Game 4 vs. the Cavs was the fifth time Jalen Duren's been held to single-digit points in the playoffs. He was held under 10 points in just six of his 70 games in the regular season.

item.title: Donovan Mitchell demolishes Pistons in the second half to even series
item.description: Danny Cunningham: #Cavs beat the Pistons 112-103 in a game that wasn't as close as the final score. Donovan Mitchell had one of his best games as a member of the Cavs, finishing with 43 points -- 39 of them in the 2nd half. Harden had 24 and hit five 3s. Series is tied at 2-2.

item.title: Game 4 vs. Detroit marks Donovan Mitchell's fourth …
item.description: ESPN Insight: Game 4 vs. Detroit marks Donovan Mitchell's fourth 40-point playoff game with the Cavaliers, passing Kyrie Irving for second-most in franchise history . Only LeBron James (24) has more.

item.title: JB Bickerstaff on what’s enabled Tobias Harris to …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB Bickerstaff on what’s enabled Tobias Harris to score 20+ in eight straight games: “His want to help this team and it’s as simple as that. He sees where a need is and he has the ability to fill that need.”

item.title: Caris LeVert is available, Kevin Huerter remains OUT
item.description: Jacob Richman: Caris LeVert is available, Kevin Huerter remains OUT.

item.title: The Cavaliers have listed Thomas Bryant (low back …
item.description: The Cavaliers have listed Thomas Bryant (low back spasms) as questionable for Monday's Game 4 against Detroit.

item.title: NBA Last Two Minute Report from Game 3 shows 0 errors. …
item.description: Chris Fedor: NBA Last Two Minute Report from Game 3 shows 0 errors. Tobias Harris’ late contest on James Harden’s 3-point dagger was clean, not considered a foul. “Harris jumps to contest Harden's shot, but would otherwise avoid making contact if not for Harden extending forward.”

item.title: Caris LeVert listed as questionable for Game 4 vs. Cavs
item.description: The Pistons have downgraded Caris LeVert (right heel contusion) to questionable for Monday’s Game 4 against Cleveland. Kevin Huerter (left adductor strain) is also questionable.

item.title: Duncan Robinson and Caris LeVert listed as questionable for Game 5
item.description: The Pistons have downgraded Caris LeVert (right heel contusion) and Duncan Robinson (low back soreness) as questionable for Wednesday’s Game 5 against Cleveland. Kevin Huerter (left adductor strain) is also questionable.

item.title: “If Grant Hill was 100% healthy, he would've never …
item.description: Run It Back: “If Grant Hill was 100% healthy, he would've never left Detroit." Tim Hardaway Sr. says Grant Hill would've been "up there" if injuries didn't derail his career

item.title: Scripps Sports has scored local rights to its first …
item.description: Scripps Sports has scored local rights to its first NBA team, as the Pistons will air games over-the-air with the company beginning with the 2026-27 season. Terms of the deal were not disclosed. Games will air in the Detroit DMA, surrounding areas and across the state of Michigan. The move to Scripps comes after the team had long been on FanDuel Sports Network Detroit, which folded following the season. This will mark the first time local Pistons games will primarily be on free TV since 2005.

item.title: Sources told SBJ that the Hornets were among the teams …
item.description: Sources told SBJ that the Hornets were among the teams considering a deal with streaming platform Victory+. Sources also told SBJ last month that Fubo makes surprise pitch to all 13 NBA teams that fled Main Street. DAZN, Gray TV and Nexstar have also been reported as local options for teams. The Pistons’ former RSN also was the home to the Tigers and Red Wings, but those clubs – both owned by the Ilitch family -- launched Detroit SportsNet in collaboration with MLB Media. That first-of-its-kind,

item.title: Starting next season, the Pistons will air their …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: BREAKING: Starting next season, the Pistons will air their preseason and regular season games for free locally on Channel 20

item.title: “They’re getting more calls than we are.”
item.description: Hunter Patterson: J.B. when asked how the whistle has changed after Kenny Atkinson’s comments: “They’re getting more calls than we are.”

item.title: “Depending on the feel of the game we give guys those …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: J.B. on Isaiah Stewart’s limited minutes: “Depending on the feel of the game we give guys those opportunities based on what their role is for us. Stew’s done what we’ve asked him to do. It’s just seemed like in those games we’ve needed a spark and P Reed has that spark ability.”

item.title: “Stew’s done what we’ve asked him to do. It just …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB denied that Stew is dealing with a physical issue. On his minutes decrease: “Stew’s done what we’ve asked him to do. It just seemed like in those games we needed a spark and P Reed has that spark ability.”

item.title: “We lost guys a few times. Watching the ball in their …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB on their defensive mistakes the last two games: “We lost guys a few times. Watching the ball in their isos and they have guys who are really good relocation shooters … we have some things to improve.”

item.title: Duncan Robinson (low back soreness) out for Game 5 against Cavaliers
item.description: Jamal Collier: Duncan Robinson (low back soreness) is OUT tonight for Detroit. Kevin Huerter is active tonight for the first time this series

item.title: Cade Cunningham, Daniss Jenkins, Ausar Thompson, …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Pistons starters: Cade Cunningham, Daniss Jenkins, Ausar Thompson, Tobias Harris, Jalen Duren

item.title: “With Cade, you cannot give him the same look. He’s …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Kenny Atkinson on defending Cade: “With Cade, you cannot give him the same look. He’s like a great quarterback, you give the same look to him he’s gonna figure it out. Keep mixing and matching different coverages, different matchups.

item.title: James Harden goes off for 30 points, eight rebounds, six assists as Cavaliers win thrilling Game 5 in overtime over Detroit
item.description: StatMuse: James Harden in Game 5 vs Detroit: 30 PTS, 8 REB, 6 AST, 3 BLK. Joins SGA and Kobe as the only guards with those numbers in a playoff game since 2010.

item.title: Cade Cunningham had a game-high 39 points on 6/10 from …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Cavs hold on to beat the Pistons 117-113 after outscoring them 14-10 in overtime. Cleveland takes a 3-2 series lead. Cade Cunningham had a game-high 39 points on 6/10 from distance, 9 assists, 7 boards and 2 steals.

item.title: Ties Isiah Thomas for the most 30 PT games by a Piston …
item.description: StatMuse: Cade Cunningham in Game 5 vs Cleveland: 39 PTS 7 REB 9 AST 2 STL Ties Isiah Thomas for the most 30 PT games by a Piston in a single playoff run.

item.title: Pistons led by 9 with 2:37 left in the 4th quarter
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Pistons led by 9 with 2:37 left in the 4th quarter. They were outscored 23-10 the through the end of the 4th & OT, shooting 4-for-17 from the field.

item.title: “You call that call anytime of the game. That call has …
item.description: NBA Courtside: P.J. Tucker and Brian Windhorst reach to the no call on Ausar Thompson last night: “You call that call anytime of the game. That call has to be made. 1000%. There’s no way you don’t make that call — I believe it was a foul. If I were a Pistons fan I wouldn’t listen to anything else”

item.title: Cade Cunningham: 'We all saw the play, I think it’s pretty clear it was a foul'
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Cade Cunningham on the final play of regulation: “We all saw the play, I think it’s pretty clear it was a foul. (Ausar) has the ball in his possession, he’s running forward, he gets tripped up. It’s a foul, it’s been a foul the whole rest of the game. Wasn’t a foul that time.”

item.title: On the late no-call against Ausar: “He fouled Ausar. …
item.description: ?Omari Sankofa II?: JB: “They started doubling and getting the ball out of Cade’s hands. We’ll find ways to fix it. We’ll be better.” On the late no-call against Ausar: “He fouled Ausar. It’s clear. He trips him when he’s going for a loose ball. End of game situation, that’s tough.”

item.title: Cade Cunningham on the mindset moving forward: “It’s …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Cade Cunningham on the mindset moving forward: “It’s first to four wins. We know that, we’ve had our back against the wall before. At the end of the day if we can’t win a game on the road, how far are we really going to get in the playoffs? … Got to go win a game on the road.”

item.title: “It’s about exploiting whatever they’re doing that’s …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Asked Cade Cunningham about the challenge of generating quality offense outside of him: “It’s about exploiting whatever they’re doing that’s giving us advantages. … I just haven’t done a good enough job of drawing the two and then getting the ball to guys cleanly.”

item.title: Cleveland outscored Detroit 23-10, shot 50% to the …
item.description: ?Kurt Helin?: In the final 3 minutes and OT, Cleveland outscored Detroit 23-10, shot 50% to the Pistons 23.5%, was 2-of-3 from 3 while Detroit was 0-of-5, and Mitchell was 4-of-5 with 9 points, while Cunningham was 1-of-4 for 2 points.

item.title: “Just sticking with it. Every single minute, every …
item.description: Evan Mobley emphasized composure and collective execution after the Cleveland Cavaliers escaped with a 117-113 overtime win over the Detroit Pistons in Game 5 of the Eastern Conference semifinals. “Just sticking with it. Every single minute, every single second counts at the end of games like this and I think we stuck with it till the very end,” Mobley said, describing the mindset behind Cleveland’s comeback from a nine-point deficit in the final minutes of regulation. The Cavaliers turned a 103

item.title: Pistons got Evan Mobley looking like Scar
item.description: ?Spencer Davies?: Pistons got Evan Mobley looking like Scar.

item.title: Max Strus: "I look better? You're saying I look …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Max Strus: "I look better? You're saying I look bigger?" "I do. I think you look like the Pistons. They got a bunch of big guys. I think you look like them" Max: "I'll take that as a compliment" ??

item.title: Jalen Duren: 'I feel I can be better in all aspects of the game'
item.description: ?Omari Sankofa II?: Duren: “Absolutely. I feel I can be better in all aspects of the game, and continue to grind and continue to grow.” On Reed closing the 4th and OT: “My brother handled it. B-Ball came in ready to go and did his thing.”

item.title: Pistons upgrade Duncan Robinson to questionable for Game 6
item.description: The Pistons have upgraded Duncan Robinson (low back soreness) to questionable for Friday's Game 6 against Cleveland. Kevin Huerter (left adductor strain) and Caris LeVert (right heel contusion) are also questionable.

item.title: L2M report says there were zero incorrect calls late in Cavaliers-Pistons game, including contact between Jarrett Allen, Ausar Thompson
item.description: Danny Cunningham: The NBA’s Last 2 Minute Report says that there were zero incorrect calls in the final two minutes of regulation and overtime in last night’s #Cavs win over the Pistons.

item.title: “Allen and Thompson legally step to the same spot …
item.description: Chris Fedor: NBA Last Two Minute Report from #Cavs-#Pistons Game 5 shows no missed calls. The review of the final play: “Allen and Thompson legally step to the same spot while pursuing the loose ball [before either player has possession] & both lose their balance from the marginal contact”

item.title: Some tipoff scenarios for Game 1 of ECF if it ends up …
item.description: Ian Begley: Some tipoff scenarios for Game 1 of ECF if it ends up being played on Sunday:

item.title: Knicks playoff run could generate $140 million for Madison Square Garden Sports
item.description: The New York Knicks have punched their ticket to the Eastern Conference finals for the second season in a row after last playing in the ECF in 2000, producing a windfall for Madison Square Garden Sports, which owns the club and the NHL’s Rangers. The Knicks have already played five playoff home games, which generated nearly $50 million in gross revenue before the NBA took its cut from ticket receipts. The team is likely in line for at least five more home games, as the heavy favorite versus whoe

item.title: Bam Adebayo, Harrison Barnes, Jaylen Brown, Tobias Harris, and Larry Nance Jr. the finalists for Social Justice Champion award
item.description: NBA: As we honor the sixth year of the NBA Social Justice Champion award, join us in recognizing Bam Adebayo, Harrison Barnes, Jaylen Brown, Tobias Harris, and Larry Nance Jr. as the finalists for the 2025-26 season. This award honors NBA Hall of Famer Kareem Abdul-Jabbar and his lifetime commitment to driving impactful change around social justice.

item.title: "He's not a max player, but they're probably going to …
item.description: With Cunningham, already on a well-earned supermax contract, at $270 million over five years, this isn't as much about playing hardball as it could be about protecting the upside of a roster loaded with other young players. "He's not a max player, but they're probably going to have to give him the max," an East executive said of Jalen Duren. "Because now [cap space] teams like Chicago or Brooklyn might see him as someone they could get with a max offer sheet and Detroit will have to match. "With

item.title: Danny Green says Detroit Pistons need to bench Jalen …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Danny Green says Detroit Pistons need to bench Jalen Duren: “I’m praying for him, I hope nothing going on whatever’s happening with Jalen Duren. Regardless of the fact we can’t play him, we got to win a playoff game if you have something going on you better communicate that we got to sit you regardless your not performing. Only thing else came to mind how do you get him to pick it up, the best translator something that computes to your brain is the bench. Bobby Knight a** on bench

item.title: Duncan Robinson, Caris LeVert and Kevin Huerter are …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Duncan Robinson, Caris LeVert and Kevin Huerter are all present at shootaround this morning.

item.title: They now have to decide what to do with Jalen Duren, …
item.description: Tim Bontemps: They now have to decide what to do with Jalen Duren, pay him this summer, and this has been a very concerning month on that front. You have to do the same thing with Ausar Thompson, and it's been frankly a very concerning month on that front too because those two guys have shown that while they are talented players, if those are your three core players going forward, that's going to be a challenge to get where you hope to go with Cade Cunningham.

item.title: Wilt: 50.4 to 35.0 in 1961-62 (-15.4)
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Biggest scoring drops in the playoffs in an All-Star year Wilt: 50.4 to 35.0 in 1961-62 (-15.4) Jalen Duren: 19.5 to 10.1 this year (-9.4) Elgin Baylor: 24.8 to 15.4 in 1968-69 (-9.4) Flynn Robinson: 21.8 to 12.8 in 1969-70 (-9.0) Julius Randle: 25.1 to 16.6 in 2022-23 (-8.5)

item.title: Duncan Robinson, Kevin Huerter, Caris LeVert will be available against Cavs
item.description: The Pistons have upgraded Duncan Robinson (low back soreness), Kevin Huerter (left adductor strain) and Caris LeVert (right heel contusion) to available for Friday's Game 6 against Cleveland.

item.title: He received an injection to play in tonight’s Game 6, …
item.description: Jamal Collier: Pistons guard Kevin Huerter re-aggravated his adductor injury during Game 5, which is why he was pulled after playing just three minutes. He received an injection to play in tonight’s Game 6, sources told ESPN, and he is listed as available.

item.title: Those guys are gonna be a huge part of what we do for …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB on changing lineups vs trusting guys to figure it out: "You’re looking at this big picture and longterm. Those guys are gonna be a huge part of what we do for a long time, and what does it say about us if in their difficult times, we choose to give up on them?"

item.title: J.B. on balancing coaching for the long term while …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: J.B. on balancing coaching for the long term while prioritizing winning now:

item.title: Bickerstaff: “Horse shit.”
item.description: Jackson Flickinger: J.B. Bickerstaff: “What did the two-minute report say?” Joe Vardon: “No foul.” Bickerstaff: “Horse shit.”

item.title: He would practice those things and work on them, even …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB on Harden, who he coached for four years in Houston: “He would foul bait me. We would play one on one. He would practice those things and work on them, even just playing one on one versus me. He has a great understanding of the rules and how to manipulate them in his favor.”

item.title: “It’s a feel. I wish I could say when he hits 32 …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Atkinson on how they decide to trap Cade: “It’s a feel. I wish I could say when he hits 32 points we’re going to start trapping. It’s a feel … at the end of the game you have to know when to go to it and when not to. Glad we got some reps at it.” Said they have to mix coverages.

item.title: “That’s a great question.”
item.description: NBA on ESPN: “That’s a great question.” — James Harden on the Cavs not playing with the intensity needed to close out the series.

item.title: Pistons take care of Cavs, force Game 7
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Pistons beat the Cavs 115-94 to force another Game 7. Back to LCA we go.

item.title: Detroit has won 4 elimination games this postseason, …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Detroit has won 4 elimination games this postseason, the most by a team in one playoff run since the Celtics in 2023. They have a +16.0 average margin of victory in elimination games this postseason, 2nd-highest in NBA history (min. 3 games).

item.title: Jeff Zillgitt: I've always enjoyed my interactions …
item.description: Who is the most memorable/ favorite interview you had with? Jeff Zillgitt: I've always enjoyed my interactions with Dwyane Wade. He has a great ability to provide a thoughtful/insightful answer. I wrote a story on Kyle Korver one year and he was just fantastic describing the checklist he goes through to make sure he's shooting the way he wants to shoot. Just so detailed. I really appreciate the players who willing to open about their lives and what they do on the court. A recent story I wrote ab

item.title: After the game, Zarba told a pool reporter the …
item.description: Lead referee Zach Zarba said Ausar Thompson’s left arm extended to Merrill’s head and neck area and “there was follow-through,” and he assessed Thompson a flagrant foul, penalty 1, which meant Thompson could stay in the game. “I just felt like it was a moving screen (on Merrill),” Thompson said. “I was just hoping that whatever (the referees) decided, I was going to be able to keep playing.” After the game, Zarba told a pool reporter the referees did see Thompson’s hand on Merrill’s neck but did

item.title: Pistons have expressed certainty that Jalen Duren …
item.description: Throughout the playoffs, the Pistons have expressed certainty that Jalen Duren would overcome his underwhelming postseason play. His biggest advocate has been Reed. “I don’t think anybody in here is as happy for JD as I am,” Reed told The Athletic inside Cleveland’s visiting locker room. “I was talking to him all night, telling him, ‘That’s the energy we need. Go harder. Make sure there’s nothing left in the tank.’‌”

item.title: Duncan Robinson, Caris LeVert, Kevin Huerter listed as questionable for Game 7
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Robinson, LeVert and Huerter all remain questionable for Game 7 tomorrow

item.title: Larry Nance Jr ruled out for Game 7 vs. Detroit
item.description: The Cavaliers have downgraded Larry Nance Jr (illness) as out for Sunday’s Game 7 against Detroit

item.title: Duncan Robinson, Kevin Huerter and Caris LeVert were …
item.description: Coty M. Davis: Duncan Robinson, Kevin Huerter and Caris LeVert were at shootaround this morning. They are all questionable for Game 7 tonight vs. the Cavs. #Pistons

item.title: Caris LeVert has been a key player for the Pistons …
item.description: Caris LeVert has been a key player for the Pistons during their 60-win season, but his greatest influence has been evident throughout the playoffs. "For me, it was all about knowing where I am in my career and knowing how good this team was," LeVert said. "It was all about getting my body right for this moment (the postseason) while realizing that there is still more room for me to grow. It's all about playing winning basketball, which has always been the most important thing for me."

item.title: "He's a pro, the way he carries himself and how he …
item.description: "He's a pro, the way he carries himself and how he handles different things being thrown at him," guard Cade Cunningham said. "He can adapt to anything, and he gets so much respect from that alone. His versatility on the court allows us to play him on and off the ball. He's been in a lot of different situations and knows what it takes."

item.title: "This is why 'Vert is here," coach J.B. Bickerstaff …
item.description: "This is why 'Vert is here," coach J.B. Bickerstaff said. "He isn't afraid of the moment. He is a two-way impactful player. He can put a ton of pressure on defenses. He has that off-rhythm cadence to his game, which makes him hard to guard. He can get to the paint and make shots. Defensively, he is a pest. He can put his hands on people. He is digging at the ball. He can get deflections, get steals, and take one-on-ones. This is why we wanted 'Vert to be with us this year, because we knew what h

item.title: Similar to Game 5 when they took eight busloads of …
item.description: Nick Camino: Dan Gilbert and the @cavs went BIG for tonight’s Game 7 in Detroit. Similar to Game 5 when they took eight busloads of season ticket holders up to Detroit for the game, today they’re bringing up 25 busloads of #Cavs fans, with all expenses covered for Game 7. Yes, TWENTY-FIVE!

item.title: I think he found something that he liked and that he …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: J.B. on whether Jalen Duren got rolling in Game 6 because Stew and Reed played early: “I’m not sure if it was the timing of those guys’ minutes. We were able to find more space for him to operate. … I think he found something that he liked and that he can replicate.”

item.title: Hope it works again tonight.”
item.description: Hunter Patterson: J.B. on starting Daniss last game: “Even with Ausar in that lineup you’re seeing there are three guys who bring the ball up. So they can’t just hone in on where (Cade) is gonna be. We can move him around more freely, the point of attack changes. … Hope it works again tonight.”

item.title: A global media panel of 100 voters selected the winner …
item.description: NBA Communications: A global media panel of 100 voters selected the winner of the 2025-26 Kia NBA Most Valuable Player Award. The complete voting results:

item.title: It wasn’t this huge thing we had to overcome. It was …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB: “For us, the focus has always just been the moment and it’s just one game. That's the way we’ve treated these playoffs since being down 3-1 against Orlando. It wasn’t this huge thing we had to overcome. It was just, you had to win one game.”

item.title: The plan is to move Dean Wade out of the starting …
item.description: Sources tell cleveland.com that coach Kenny Atkinson is likely to make a starting lineup change for Game 7 of the Eastern Conference semifinals against the Detroit Pistons Sunday night. The plan is to move Dean Wade out of the starting lineup, swapping in battle-tested veteran Max Strus.

item.title: Duncan Robinson, Caris LeVert and Kevin Huerter are …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Duncan Robinson, Caris LeVert and Kevin Huerter are all available for the Pistons tonight.

item.title: Kenny Atkinson on Cavs adjustments made: We were avoiding Ausar Thompson
item.description: MrBuckBuck: Kenny Atkinson on the Cleveland Cavaliers' adjustments: "The tactical adjustment we made was avoiding [Ausar] Thompson. We were just like, if he's near the ball, throw it to someone else." Via: https:// reddit.com/r/nba/comments /1tgauk0/kenny_atkinson_on_the_cleveland_cavaliers/

item.title: "That game sucked."
item.description: ESPN: "That game sucked." Cade Cunningham on the Pistons' Game 7 loss at home against the Cavs.

item.title: “Obviously a disappointing series right here. Fell …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Cade Cunningham on this season: “Obviously a disappointing series right here. Fell short of doing enough of the right things to win the series. But we were the No. 1 seed for a reason.”

item.title: There was a great opportunity to move forward and to …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Cade on if this loss will fuel them: "A lot. There was a great opportunity to move forward and to continue to experience new things that we hadn't been exposed to. Fell short. We were right there but we fell short. That chip and that sting, we're going to feel for a while."

item.title: We didn't come out with the right disposition for a …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Robinson: "I don't want to be over-reactionary having not watched it ... we didn't come out with the right disposition for a Game 7 and that was apparent in all the areas we prided ourselves on this year, areas we thought we could compete with and win, we didn't win those."

item.title: Jarrett Allen said that #Cavs owner Dan Gilbert sent …
item.description: Danny Cunningham: Jarrett Allen said that #Cavs owner Dan Gilbert sent him a message of encouragement before Game 7.

item.title: JB Bickerstaff on if he's disappointed: 'That adjective will never be used with this group'
item.description: Hunter Patterson: J.B. on whether this season was a disappointment: “It’s not a disappointment at all. Not ever will I be disappointed in these guys. These guys, every single day, give us what they’ve got. … It’s a loss, it’s a tough loss. But that adjective will never be used with this group.”

item.title: Jalen Duren averaged 9.3 fewer points in the playoffs …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Jalen Duren averaged 9.3 fewer points in the playoffs than he did in the regular season, the biggest drop off in an All-Star season since Elgin Baylor in 1969.

item.title: Cavs blowout Pistons in Game 7, will face Knicks in conference finals
item.description: Danny Cunningham: #Cavs dominate the Detroit Pistons 125-94 to advance to the Eastern Conference Finals. Donovan Mitchell was excellent, Jarrett Allen and Evan Mobley were too, and Sam Merrill was outstanding off the bench. As dominant of a performance as they've had this year.

item.title: Their deepest run since 2008 ends with an incredibly …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: FINAL: Cavaliers 125, Pistons 94. The Pistons have been eliminated from the playoffs. Their deepest run since 2008 ends with an incredibly flat, disappointing performance on their home floor. Lots to build off of this season, but this was a nightmare way for it to end.

item.title: This will be the first time this season where Detroit …
item.description: Bobby Marks: This will be the first time this season where Detroit will have lost consecutive home games.

item.title: The Cleveland faithful were chanting “Let’s go, Cavs” …
item.description: Gilbert’s plans worked to perfection. As previously mentioned, Allen controlled the game. He (and, to be fair, Evan Mobley) set the tone on the interior, and Mitchell was masterful with his 26 points, eight assists, and seven boards. Sam Merrill made five 3s off the bench. The Cavs blew the doors off. And so did their fans. With the rout fully underway late in the third quarter and Detroit fans not having left quite yet, the Cleveland faithful were chanting “Let’s go, Cavs” loud enough that it c

item.title: JB Bickerstaff safe
item.description: Although Detroit collapsed in the second round, the front office’s confidence in JB Bickerstaff, who signed a contract extension the day after the Pistons ousted the Magic in the first round, hasn’t wavered. The Pistons view him as the coach of the future, and the expectation, per league sources, is for Bickerstaff to be around long term. He’s established meaningful relationships throughout the locker room and has cultivated a culture that the organization believes is sustainable, given the team

item.title: Tobias Harris expected to have strong free-agent market
item.description: Veteran Tobias Harris was Detroit’s second scoring option. The 33-year-old, whom Bickerstaff has routinely referred to as his “safety blanket,” averaged 18.1 points per game, good for the second most in his playoff career. But Harris scored a combined 11 points on 2-of-13 shooting in the final two games of the second round. Harris will be an unrestricted free agent this offseason. As valuable as Harris was to start the postseason, he couldn’t sustain his level of offensive production as the play

item.title: Ausar Thompson is also extension eligible. Per a …
item.description: Ausar Thompson is also extension eligible. Per a league source, Detroit views Thompson as part of its core and will seek to get a rookie-scale extension done with the third-year wing in the offseason. Thompson tallied more “stocks” than anyone in the postseason (28 steals and 25 blocks), and his impact was undeniable despite his offensive limitations. “People can judge however they want to judge, but you’re looking at this big picture and long term,” Bickerstaff said ahead of Game 6 when asked i

item.title: Trajan Langdon will host an end of season media …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Trajan Langdon will host an end of season media availability tomorrow at 1 pm.

item.title: They likely will be connected to big names around the …
item.description: They likely will be connected to big names around the league, such as Utah Jazz forward Lauri Markkanen − though Utah just traded for Jaren Jackson Jr. to pair with Markkanen − and Los Angeles Clippers superstar Kawhi Leonard, who will be entering the final year of his deal and will make $50.3 million in 2026-27 at age 35. The Pistons have all of their first-round picks, including the 21st overall pick in the 2026 NBA Draft and a trove of second-round picks down the road to include in deals.

item.title: Ausar Thompson is also extension eligible. Per a …
item.description: Ausar Thompson is also extension eligible. Per a league source, Detroit views Thompson as part of its core and will seek to get a rookie-scale extension done with the third-year wing in the offseason. Thompson tallied more “stocks” than anyone in the postseason (28 steals and 25 blocks), and his impact was undeniable despite his offensive limitations.

item.title: No max money for Jalen Duren?
item.description: While his second postseason experience was undoubtedly a disappointment, Detroit will try to retain the Philadelphia native this offseason — just at a lower rate than expected. Duren could make an All-NBA team, which would make him eligible for a five-year max contract worth 30 percent of the salary cap. Given his postseason showing, the widely held belief around the league is that he has little to no leverage to negotiate for the max.

item.title: The Pistons view him as the coach of the future, and …
item.description: Although Detroit collapsed in the second round, the front office’s confidence in Bickerstaff, who signed a contract extension the day after the Pistons ousted the Magic in the first round, hasn’t wavered. The Pistons view him as the coach of the future, and the expectation, per league sources, is for Bickerstaff to be around long term. He’s established meaningful relationships throughout the locker room and has cultivated a culture that the organization believes is sustainable, given the team’s

item.title: Pistons president on Jalen Duren: We look forward to getting a deal done and for him to continue being a Piston
item.description: Michael Scotto: Detroit Pistons President Trajan Langdon on Jalen Duren: “JD had a fantastic season. All-Star. One of the biggest contributors to us being a No. 1 seed… We look forward to coming together with his representation, getting a deal done, and for him continuing to be a Piston.”

item.title: Pistons hope to re-sign Tobias Harris: His professionalism has been huge for us
item.description: Michael Scotto: Trajan Langdon on Tobias Harris: “His approach and professionalism has been huge in terms of us establishing the environment we have in the past two years… They call him Unc for a reason… We hope that we can bring him back. He’s somebody we want to keep in a Pistons uniform.”

item.title: "JD had a fantastic season. All-Star, one of the …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: On Duren, Langdon sounds confident they'll extend him in some capacity. "JD had a fantastic season. All-Star, one of the biggest contributors to us getting a one seed … an incredible leap from last year to this year because of the work he put in last summer and his focus."

item.title: "I think defenses took some things away from him and …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Langdon on Duren's postseason: "I think defenses took some things away from him and that's the biggest thing ... I think he would say I didn’t play the basketball I would’ve liked to have played. Sometimes that happens in the league with young players."

item.title: "I don’t think there was a rock that was not …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Langdon of if they were "seduced" by their regular season and didn't do enough at the trade deadline: "I don’t think there was a rock that was not overturned as far as understanding where we were ... some things were way too expensive at that point, we felt."

item.title: Says Ausar spent a lot of time last summer on his …
item.description: Ku: Trajan Langdon says Ausar Thompson was "big time" and he had an incredible season. Says that he impacts the game in both seasons. Says Ausar spent a lot of time last summer on his offensive game, but had restrictions all summer. Says this summer will not have any restrictions, he will work even more on his offensive game, and he will be even better next year.

item.title: "I thought Cade was an absolute warrior. I haven’t had …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Langdon: "I thought Cade was an absolute warrior. I haven’t had a collapsed lung, I’m sure most of you haven’t, and he had a collapsed lung and fractured rib." Noted he had to sit on the couch for 7-10 days to heal and went through deconditioning right as they ramped up for playoffs.

item.title: "I know it frustrated him a lot because he didn’t get …
item.description: Omari Sanokfa II: Langdon said Cade will learn lot from the playoffs, and his lung injury: "I know it frustrated him a lot because he didn’t get to be his best self. Nobody can in that situation. He’ll learn from it. He’s probably going to look at everything and say I have to be more efficient."

item.title: "Did I think it would equal 60 wins? Even after the …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Langdon said he knew the team could be good after the 13-game winning streak: "Did I think it would equal 60 wins? Even after the winning streak I didn’t think it would be 60 wins and that’s an incredible feat, one that hadn't been done here in nearly 20 years."

item.title: "He’s a better player now than when we got him off …
item.description: Omari Sanokfa II: Langdon on Paul Reed: "Consummate professional. In and out of the lineup this season and it didn’t matter if his number was called." "He’s a better player now than when we got him off waivers two years ago."

item.title: Trajan says he believes Ron Holland losing his spot in …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Trajan says he believes Ron Holland losing his spot in the rotation will motivate him over the summer and thinks Ron will improve next season because of that frustration. Noted how helpful Ron was to the Pistons winning 60 games during the regular season.

item.title: Josh Hart before Game 1: 'These 20-point leads right now at this point in the season, they literally mean nothing'
item.description: Josh Hart: "And I think with this year, you're seeing these 20-point leads dwindle fast. I mean, how many times is Detroit came back in Orlando being down 20 a couple games. You've seen Philly's done it, Minnesota just did it last night and then they erased like a 20-point lead, then got tied and then gave up, you know, gave up and lost by like 20 again. But these 20-point leads right now at this point in the season, they literally mean nothing. So that's why you have to continue that, you know,

item.title: Stephon Castle joins Sleepy Floyd, Cade Cunningham, …
item.description: Justin Russo: Stephon Castle joins Sleepy Floyd, Cade Cunningham, and James Harden as the only players in league history to have 20 turnovers during any 2-game stretch. Castle the first to do it in the postseason.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama headlines 2025-26 All-Defensive Teams
item.description: NBA Communications: The 2025-26 Kia NBA All-Defensive First Team: ▪️ Rudy Gobert, @Timberwolves ▪️ Chet Holmgren, @okcthunder ▪️ Ausar Thompson, @DetroitPistons ▪️ Victor Wembanyama, @spurs ▪️ Derrick White, @celtics
